blocks.content: The east side was quiet at this hour — past breakfast but before the heat settled in. They rode four across on the wide blocks, fell into pairs on the narrow ones.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: —the saddle has to still be there because the Crenshaws said it was there last spring and nobody's been in since—

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: Nobody who would take a saddle. A saddle is a very specific thing to take. You have to want a saddle. You don't just stumble out of an abandoned barn with one under your arm.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: You don't know what people take.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I know what people don't take. They don't take saddles.
He looked at her. The morning light caught the edge of her hair.
She nodded and they rode the last two blocks without saying anything, and behind them Eli was still explaining the logistics of removing a saddle from an abandoned barn.
